# Ant Code v3.0 Dashboard Acceptance

Date: 2026-05-17

## Scope

This record defines the current Ant Code v3.0 release line. Version 3.0 is the
first Dashboard/WebUI product-generation release: `ant-code` still defaults to
the mature TUI, while `ant-code dashboard` provides the local browser surface
for lab users who prefer a WebUI.

## Version Lineage

The current version number is product-lineage based, not a simple continuation
of the old package number:

| Version line | Product generation | Evidence |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 1.x | Claude Code source-derived AntCode reference and clean-room base acceptance | Phase 0 old-repo audit, `docs/deployment/v1.0-acceptance.md`, and `docs/deployment/v1.1-experience-acceptance.md`. |
| 2.x | Self-owned clean-room architecture with production TUI and local extension/orchestration work | `docs/deployment/v1.2-tui-acceptance.md`, `docs/deployment/v1.3-agent-extension-acceptance.md`, and `docs/deployment/v1.4-orchestration-acceptance.md`. |
| 3.x | Dashboard/WebUI release line over the shared local session runtime | Current Dashboard records in `PROJECT_CHANGELOG.zh-CN.md` from 2026-05-13 onward. |

The old source-derived project is not used as an active implementation
workspace. It is retained only through restricted audit and handoff notes under
`docs/handoff/2026-04-29-tui-continuation`.

## Accepted Dashboard Behavior

- Dashboard starts with `ant-code dashboard`, binds to loopback, and defaults to
  port `7410` with automatic port fallback.
- Dashboard reuses the same core session runtime, permission engine, model
  gateway, context compaction, and `.lab-agent/sessions` history as the TUI.
- Dashboard history renders only user and final assistant messages by default,
  while tool activity remains folded into concise activity rows.
- Long history is loaded on demand from transcript chunks. Opening a session
  renders the newest 100 visible messages; scrolling to the top loads earlier
  pages.
- Running session refresh and SSE reconnect use event sequences and turn ids to
  avoid duplicating user messages, final replies, or draft content.
- Assistant draft display is turn-scoped so interrupted or guided drafts do not
  leak into the next user message.
- Sidebar session actions include refresh feedback, copy session id, and guarded
  delete for non-running sessions.
- Dashboard file previews cover images, text, Markdown, code, PDF, and
  first-release Office/binary cards.
- Right-bottom change statistics show per-turn net file/addition/deletion
  counts instead of accumulating intermediate tool-call diffs.
- Dashboard can attach images by file picker or paste, sends them only for the
  current model turn, and persists only redacted image metadata in session
  history.
- Dashboard model status shows the current model plus text/image/thinking
  capability labels. The model selector can switch among registered same-gateway
  models and can save local gateway/model/subagent/visual-agent settings.
- Registered model entries can declare `modalities` and per-model subagent
  defaults. Visual image fallback uses `agents.vision` and
  `agents.modelTiers.vision` within the same active gateway.
- If the selected main model is text-only and a same-gateway visual model is
  configured, Dashboard image turns are preprocessed by the `visual-verifier`
  profile and the main model receives a bounded visual evidence report instead
  of raw image blocks.
- If no same-gateway visual model is available, image turns are blocked with an
  explicit unsupported-vision error instead of silently sending unusable input.
- Background subagent groups are visible in the live-status strip after the
  parent turn ends. `subagent_group_started`, `subagent_group_progress`, and
  `subagent_group_wakeup` are mapped without exposing the wake prompt body to
  the browser activity object.
- For `background=true` and `wakeParent=true`, Dashboard consumes the completed
  group wake prompt through an internal `wakeup` queue item. Busy parent turns
  queue the continuation; idle parent sessions auto-run it. Task group records
  include `wakePromptConsumedAt` after Dashboard consumes the prompt.
- Composer, queue, demand-confirmation, permission, context, and guide controls
  are compact enough for long running conversations: confirmation panels have a
  single internal scroll area, permission text is inline, running queue help is
  inline, and the guide top action appears only when typed guidance can affect
  the running task.

## Verification

Latest targeted verification before the v3.0 version cut:

```powershell
node --check src\core\session.js
node --check src\tools\diff.js
node --check src\tools\file-tools.js
node --check src\dashboard\events.js
node --check src\core\events.js
node --check src\dashboard\sessions.js
node --check src\dashboard\public\app.js
node --test tests\unit\dashboard-events.test.js tests\unit\dashboard-files.test.js tests\unit\dashboard-markdown.test.js tests\unit\dashboard-rich-assets.test.js tests\unit\dashboard-structured-data.test.js tests\unit\dashboard-permissions.test.js tests\unit\dashboard-runtime.test.js tests\unit\dashboard-server.test.js tests\unit\dashboard-ui.test.js tests\unit\storage.test.js tests\unit\tools.test.js
node --test tests\unit\session.test.js tests\unit\events.test.js tests\unit\tools.test.js
git diff -- src\cli\tui.js src\cli\tui
npm run build:exe
.\dist\ant-code-windows-x64\ant-code.exe --version
```

Expected version output for this release line:

```text
ant-code 3.0.0
```

## Known Limitations

- Dashboard is local-only and intentionally does not provide LAN sharing.
- The TUI remains the default `ant-code` entrypoint; Dashboard changes must not
  rewrite TUI interaction behavior.
- Live gateway evidence still belongs to the lab operator before broad
  model-enabled rollout.
- Dashboard model management is single-gateway. It does not mix one provider key
  for text with another provider key for vision in a single session.
- Model modality labels are user/config declared. The gateway/provider remains
  the source of truth for whether a named model truly accepts image input.

## Rollback

Use the previous Windows executable release directory or relink the prior
checkout commit. Local session metadata remains under `.lab-agent/sessions` and
is shared between TUI and Dashboard.
